title: Cairo ⇄ Aswan
summary: The entire length of the Nile — thirteen nights linking the Pyramids of Giza with the temples of Aswan, the most complete dahabiya voyage we offer.
description: Discover the whole river aboard a traditional dahabiya, towed for the long northern reaches and sailing the classic south. From Giza and the Fayoum through Middle Egypt's hidden tombs to Abydos, Dendera, Thebes, Edfu, Kom Ombo, and Aswan — daily international buffet dining and the signature sunset tea on deck throughout.
routeOverview: Thirteen nights from the Mediterranean delta to the Nubian south — Giza, Fayoum, Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, Abydos and Dendera, the monuments of Luxor, then Edfu, Kom Ombo, and the island temple of Philae at Aswan. Sailable Cairo → Aswan (southbound, against the current) or Aswan → Cairo (northbound, with the current).
insights: This is a traditional dahabiya towed by a support tugboat for the long northern reaches — sailing times are far longer than conventional cruisers, for an authentic, leisurely passage.
Thirteen nights covers the entire navigable Nile, combining Middle Egypt's rarely-seen monuments with the classic temples of the south aboard a single vessel.

routes.label: Cairo → Aswan
routes.overview: Sailing southbound against the Nile current aboard a towed dahabiya, this direction builds gradually from the Pyramids of Giza through Middle Egypt toward the great temples of the south, ending amid the islands of Aswan.

timeline.title: Arrival in Cairo
timeline.description: Arrive in Cairo at the start of the river.

schedule.text: Transfer to your hotel or directly to your dahabiya for embarkation.
Welcome dinner on board.
Cairo.

timeline.title: Cairo Sightseeing
timeline.description: The Pyramids of Giza and the Egyptian Museum.

schedule.text: Explore the legendary Pyramids of Giza and the treasures of the Egyptian Museum.
All meals on board, including sunset tea with snacks.
Cairo.

timeline.title: Sail to Beni Suef
timeline.description: The first long sail south through Middle Egypt.

schedule.text: South to Beni Suef — approx. 120 km · 17–18 hrs against the current.
Beni Suef.

timeline.title: Fayoum Excursion
timeline.description: Two of Egypt's earliest pyramids.

schedule.text: The ancient Hawara and Meidum pyramids.
Sunset tea and dinner on board.
Beni Suef.

timeline.title: Sail to Minya — Tuna el-Gebel & Ashmunein
timeline.description: South to Minya, with two Middle-Egypt sites.

schedule.text: On to Minya — approx. 145 km · 20–21 hrs against the current.
Tuna el-Gebel and Ashmunein.
Minya.

timeline.title: Tell el-Amarna & Beni Hassan
timeline.description: The painted tombs of Middle Egypt.

schedule.text: Tell el-Amarna — the Northern Tombs and the Tomb of Ahmose (EA3).

Minya.

timeline.title: Sail to Sohag via Asyut

schedule.text: South to Sohag via Asyut — approx. 225 km · 31–33 hrs against the current.

schedule.text: Excursion to the magnificent Temple of Abydos.
South toward Qena — approx. 160 km · 22–23 hrs against the current.

On to Luxor — approx. 65 km · 9–10 hrs against the current.

timeline.description: The wonders of ancient Thebes, both banks.

schedule.text: The Valley of the Kings and the mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut.
The Karnak Temple complex and Luxor Temple.
Luxor.

timeline.title: Sail to Edfu
timeline.description: Continue south toward Edfu.

schedule.text: South to Edfu — approx. 110 km · 15–17 hrs against the current.

timeline.description: Two southern temples, then on to Aswan.

schedule.text: Visit the impressive Temple of Edfu.
Sail to Kom Ombo for its unique double temple.
On to Aswan — approx. 105 km · 14–15 hrs against the current.
Farewell dinner on board · Aswan.

timeline.description: The treasures of the south.

schedule.text: The remarkable Aswan High Dam, the island Temple of Philae, and the fascinating Nubian Museum.
Aswan.

timeline.title: Disembarkation & Departure
timeline.description: Farewell to the river.

routes.label: Aswan → Cairo
routes.overview: Fly south to begin at Aswan, then sail home northbound with the Nile current — past the southern temples, the monuments of Thebes, and Middle Egypt's hidden tombs, ending at the Pyramids of Giza.

timeline.title: Arrival in Cairo
timeline.description: Arrive in Cairo and overnight before flying south.

schedule.text: Transfer to your hotel.
Cairo.

timeline.title: Fly to Aswan & Embarkation
timeline.description: Fly south to begin the river journey at Aswan.

schedule.text: Flight to Aswan.
Embark your dahabiya.
Aswan.

timeline.description: The treasures of the south.

schedule.text: The High Dam, the island Temple of Philae, and the Nubian Museum.

timeline.description: North to two southern temples.

schedule.text: Kom Ombo and its unique double temple, dedicated to Sobek and Horus.
On to Edfu — approx. 105 km · 12–13 hrs with partial current assistance.

timeline.description: The temple of Horus, then on to Thebes.

schedule.text: Visit the Temple of Edfu.
On to Luxor — approx. 110 km · 13–14 hrs.
Luxor.

timeline.title: Luxor Sightseeing
timeline.description: The wonders of ancient Thebes, both banks.

schedule.text: The Valley of the Kings and the Temple of Hatshepsut.
The Karnak Temple complex and Luxor Temple.
Luxor.

timeline.title: Sail to Qena — Abydos & Dendera
timeline.description: North to two sacred cult temples.

schedule.text: The temples of Abydos and Dendara.
North to Qena — approx. 65 km · 7½–8 hrs with the current.

timeline.title: Sail to Sohag via Naga Hammadi
timeline.description: A full sailing day north.

schedule.text: North past the Naga Hammadi barrage to Sohag — approx. 160 km · 18–19 hrs with the current.
Sohag.

timeline.title: Sail to Minya

schedule.text: North to Minya — approx. 225 km · 26–28 hrs with the current.
Minya.

timeline.description: A day among Middle Egypt's monuments.

schedule.text: Tell el-Amarna and its Northern Tombs, including the Tomb of Ahmose.
Tuna el-Gebel and Ashmunein.
Minya.

timeline.description: The rock-cut tombs, then north.

schedule.text: The rock-cut tombs of Beni Hassan.
North to Beni Suef — approx. 145 km · 16–18 hrs with the current.
Beni Suef.

timeline.title: Fayoum Excursion — Sail to Cairo
timeline.description: Egypt's earliest pyramids, then the capital.

schedule.text: The Hawara and Meidum pyramids.
On to Cairo — approx. 120 km · 14–15 hrs with the current.
Cairo.

timeline.title: Cairo Sightseeing
timeline.description: The Pyramids of Giza and the Egyptian Museum.

schedule.text: Tour the Pyramids of Giza and the Egyptian Museum.

timeline.description: Farewell to the river.

faq.q: Is this the most complete Nile itinerary?
faq.a: Yes — thirteen nights covers the entire navigable Nile between Cairo and Aswan, combining Middle Egypt's rarely-seen monuments with the classic temples of the south, all aboard a single traditional dahabiya.

faq.q: How does the towing work, and why?
faq.a: For the long northern reaches a support tugboat tows the dahabiya, allowing an authentic, leisurely cruise over distances no sailing dahabiya could cover alone. Southbound (Cairo → Aswan) runs against the current and is around 15–20% slower than the northbound direction.

Both visit the same sites. Cairo → Aswan builds gradually toward the southern temples; Aswan → Cairo flies you south to begin and sails home with the current. We tailor the direction to your flights and preferences.
